patterns.name: Scenario Planning Walkthrough
patterns.purpose: To stress-test current strategies against multiple plausible futures.
thinking_questions: What are the two most critical uncertainties driving the future of this space?
If we plot those uncertainties on an X/Y axis, what are the four resulting scenarios?
Does our current strategy survive in all four quadrants?
stakeholder_questions: Which stakeholders gain the most power in our worst-case scenario?
Are we relying on the cooperation of stakeholders whose incentives will change in the future?
second_order_questions: If our most optimistic scenario comes true, what new operational bottlenecks will we hit immediately?
ai_prompts: Based on these uncertainties: [A] and [B], generate a highly detailed narrative for the worst-case scenario quadrant.
Act as a hostile competitor operating in the future scenario where [Trend] accelerates. What is your strategy?
reflection_questions: Are we actively preparing for the future, or just planning for the present to continue indefinitely?
confidence_questions: How confident are you in your conclusion?
What evidence would increase your confidence?
What evidence could change your mind?

patterns.name: Literature Gap Analysis
patterns.purpose: To systematically identify what is known, what is contested, and what is missing in a body of evidence.
when_to_use: Evaluating the novelty of a proposed solution
thinking_questions: What is the consensus view in this field?
What are the major points of contention among credible experts?
What assumptions is everyone in the field making without questioning?
stakeholder_questions: Who is funding the majority of the existing research?
Whose lived experiences are entirely absent from the literature?
second_order_questions: If we fill this research gap, what adjacent fields will be forced to update their models?
ai_prompts: Summarize the major points of contention between [Theory A] and [Theory B].
Review these research abstracts and identify common methodological limitations across all of them: [Abstracts].
reflection_questions: Are you looking for a gap in the research, or just looking for evidence to support the gap you already want to fill?
confidence_questions: How confident are you in your conclusion?
What evidence would increase your confidence?
What evidence could change your mind?
